comment: Master curation table for all publications. slug = stable id for art files (assets/pub-art/papers/<slug>.png). topics = hand-tagged, primary first (legend: whatsapp, misinfo, polar, ai, health, media, methods, platforms). metaphor = illustration brief fed to generate_pub_art.py (risograph house style). match = exact title in data.js (after typo fixes).

papers.match: Inferential Privacy Leakage in Anonymized Conversational AI Logs
papers.metaphor: A paper silhouette of a person made entirely of chat transcript lines, held up to a desk lamp by a hand with a magnifying glass; hidden watermark shapes glow through the paper — a house, a birthday cake, a briefcase — secrets showing through anonymized text.

papers.match: How People Use ChatGPT: Conversation-Level Evidence from India, Nigeria, Brazil and Pakistan
papers.metaphor: A world map at night with four glowing homes on different continents, each sending a curling paper speech bubble along dotted routes to the same big friendly robot head at the center, which holds four different tools: a ladle, a textbook, a wrench and a stethoscope.

papers.match: WhatsViral: A Privacy-Preserving System for Studying Viral Content on WhatsApp
papers.metaphor: A doctor pressing a stethoscope against a giant sealed envelope with a padlock; the sound waves picked up are drawn as rows of tiny multiplying chat bubbles on a paper chart spooling out of the stethoscope, the envelope never opened.

papers.match: The Hype vs. the Reality of Generative AI in Elections: The Case of the 2024 Indian Elections
papers.metaphor: A giant parade balloon shaped like a menacing robot looming over a town square, casting a big shadow — but below it stands a small, calm, solid ballot box, and a child holds a pin on a stick up toward the balloon.

papers.match: Designing and Evaluating a Community Fact-Checking System for WhatsApp
papers.metaphor: Neighbors gathered around a village well; instead of water they draw up a bucket full of folded paper notes, and one person holds a note up to the sunlight to reveal a checkmark watermark while others wait their turn.

papers.match: Data Donation on Social Media: Tools and Datasets
papers.metaphor: A blood-donation clinic for phones: a smartphone reclines on a donor chair, a thin tube carrying tiny droplet-shaped hearts from the phone into a shared glass flask on a stand, a nurse holding a clipboard with a checklist of empty boxes.

papers.match: How Students (Really) Use ChatGPT: Uncovering Experiences Among Undergraduate Students
papers.metaphor: A student's messy desk at midnight — stacked textbooks, cold coffee, desk lamp — with a small genie made of light emerging from the laptop like from a magic lamp, offering a finished scroll; the student takes it but edits it heavily with a red pencil.

papers.match: Demographic Divides in Political Content Exposure on Facebook
papers.metaphor: One apartment building at night with a single antenna on the roof splitting into two cables; through two neighboring windows, two residents watch different glowing feeds pour from the same antenna — one stream vermilion, one indigo.

papers.match: Examining LLM Assisted Rule Creation in WhatsApp Groups
papers.metaphor: A long scroll of house rules where the top half is loose human handwriting squiggles and the bottom half is perfectly regular machine-type marks, the two halves stitched together with visible thread; a large magnifying glass inspects the seam.

papers.match: From Retrieval to Synthesis: Repair Literacy and the Domestication of Generative AI
papers.metaphor: A cozy home workshop bench where a person with a small screwdriver repairs a cuckoo clock shaped like a friendly robot head, its gears and springs laid out neatly; on the shelf above, glass jars hold spare gears and folded speech bubbles.

papers.match: Source Coverage and Citation Bias in LLM-based vs. Traditional Search Engines
papers.metaphor: Two librarians answering the same request side by side: a classic librarian high on a wooden ladder gathering an armful of varied books from many shelves, and a sleek robot librarian at a counter handing over three identical shiny volumes; thin citation threads trail from every book.

papers.match: What Does a Neighborhood Look Like Online? Linking the Built Environment to Digital Information Diets
papers.metaphor: A street of small houses with laundry lines and trees, mirrored upside-down in the sky above as the same street rebuilt from glowing chat windows and signal towers, the two versions connected by telephone poles that pass through both worlds.

papers.match: Creating Group Rules with AI: Human-AI Collaboration in WhatsApp Moderation
papers.blurb: 20 Indian group admins co-wrote rules with an AI assistant. They liked the lighter load — and drew sharp lines around trust, tone and authority.
papers.metaphor: A large community noticeboard holding one big scroll of rules, being written simultaneously by a human hand holding a pencil from the left and a friendly robot hand holding a pencil from the right, while a small crowd of round chat-bubble characters watches attentively from below like villagers at a town meeting.

papers.match: Large-Scale Multimodal Content Analysis and Annotation with Vision-Language Models
papers.blurb: A VLM toolkit that reads text, images and video in one frame — showing that text-only pipelines systematically undercount political content on WhatsApp.
papers.metaphor: A tangled flood of mixed media — photographs, film strips, posters, memes and speech bubbles — pouring into a large triangular glass prism from the left, and emerging on the right as neat, orderly parallel sorted ribbons, like chaos refracted into order.

papers.match: Quantifying the Illicit Ecosystem of Betting Apps in India
papers.blurb: Tens of thousands of Meta ads, influencer posts and 300k Play Store reviews, linked into one map from recruitment narratives to financial harm.
papers.metaphor: A giant smartphone drawn as a casino slot machine, its three reels showing a cricket ball, a coin and a pair of dice, with a fishing line and hook dangling a shiny coin above it as bait, while a stream of small coins drains out of the bottom of the phone into a dark hole.

papers.match: Evaluating Community-Based and Peer Fact-Checking on WhatsApp
papers.blurb: A randomized field study on participants' real group messages in India: peer corrections modestly outperform local journalists and national fact-checking tiplines.
papers.metaphor: Two neighbors leaning over a garden fence made of chat bubbles, one passing a crumpled rumor note to the other, who inspects it through a large magnifying glass that reveals a bold checkmark inside the note, warm and friendly neighborhood scene.

papers.match: Disagreement Is Disappearing on U.S. Cable Debate Shows
papers.blurb: 21,000 episodes and 2.13M host–guest exchanges from Fox, MSNBC and CNN: on-air debate fell by roughly a third since 2017, and pushback is partisan and asymmetric.
papers.metaphor: Two vintage television sets facing each other like boxers in a ring, each screen showing a talking head in profile, with a once-mighty lightning bolt between them now dissolving into faint dots and confetti, the spark of debate literally fading away.

papers.match: Personal Experience and Public Support for Internet Shutdowns in Rural India
papers.blurb: 246 rural participants voluntarily went offline for 48 hours. Support for shutdowns did not drop — it ticked up, as people rediscovered family time and offline life.
papers.metaphor: A rural Indian landscape at dusk where a giant wifi symbol sets on the horizon like the sun, a cleanly cut telephone cable in the foreground sprouts a small flower from its cut end, and a family sits together under a large banyan tree playing a board game by lantern light.

papers.match: Structural Dynamics of Harmful Content Dissemination on WhatsApp
papers.blurb: 5.1 million messages across ~6,000 Indian WhatsApp groups: harmful content cascades deeper and wider than benign content, and network structure — not just modality — drives the spread.
papers.metaphor: A branching cascade diagram drawn as ink dropped into water: from a single phone at the top, one dark ink drop splits and spreads downward through many layers of round chat bubbles, reaching far deeper and wider than a small tidy light-colored drop cascading beside it.

papers.match: Calibrated and Diverse News Coverage
papers.metaphor: An editor holding an old-fashioned balance scale at arm's length: both pans stacked with folded newspapers in many different patterns and colors, perfectly level, while a small dial at the scale's pivot points exactly to the middle.

papers.match: Dynamics of Toxicity in Political Podcasts
papers.metaphor: Two vintage radio microphones facing each other across a small table; from one, a wisp of dark smoke rises and curls into thorny bramble shapes, while a round meter between the microphones creeps into the red zone.

papers.match: Political Manipulation of the Israel-Hamas Conflict on WhatsApp in India
papers.metaphor: A pair of puppeteer hands hovering above a phone screen, pulling fine strings tied to a parade of chat bubbles below, the bubbles marching in tight formation carrying tiny banners and torches like a staged procession.

papers.match: Global Patterns of Viral Content on WhatsApp
papers.metaphor: A globe wrapped in one continuous garland of paper chat bubbles strung like paper dolls, circling the continents; a few bubbles burst into small confetti clouds as the chain crosses each ocean.

papers.match: Analyzing Patterns and Influence of Advertising in Print Newspapers
papers.metaphor: A broadsheet newspaper page seen from above as a map: the advertisements are bright vermilion islands slowly flooding like a rising tide over the indigo columns of news text, while a tiny journalist stands on a shrinking text island holding a pen like a flag.

papers.match: Community-Driven Fact-Checking on WhatsApp: Who Fact-Checks Whom, Why, and With What Effect?
papers.metaphor: A neighborhood wall at dusk pasted with layers of chat-bubble posters; one neighbor shines a hand torch across them and the false posters curl and peel under the beam while other neighbors point them out to each other.

papers.match: Private Sharing of Public Content on Facebook
papers.metaphor: A public town notice board covered in big bright posters, and beside it a quiet queue of people folding small copies of those posters into envelopes and slipping them one by one through a narrow mail slot in a plain door.

papers.match: WhatsApp Explorer: A Data Donation Tool To Facilitate Research on WhatsApp
papers.metaphor: An explorer with a headlamp and a butterfly net standing at the mouth of a cave shaped like a giant speech bubble, gently catching folded paper messages that flutter out like bats into specimen jars strapped to their backpack.

papers.match: Hate Speech Campaigns in the 2016 Philippine Elections on Facebook
papers.metaphor: A megaphone lashed to a bamboo pole above a town plaza raining down sharp paper darts folded from speech bubbles, while people below open banana-leaf umbrellas and huddle together under them.

papers.match: Collecting WhatsApp Data for Social Science Research: Challenges and a Proposed Solution
papers.metaphor: A researcher in a field apron harvesting padlocked envelopes that hang from a tree like fruit, placing each carefully into a woven basket lined with cotton wool, a clipboard and consent forms leaning against the trunk.

papers.match: Generative AI and Political Discourse: Insights from WhatsApp in Rural India
papers.metaphor: A rural Indian market stall at dusk selling glowing paper masks of smiling politician faces, freshly printed by a small humming machine under the cart that spits them out on a conveyor, while shoppers hold them up to their phones.

papers.match: Television Discourse Decoded: Comprehensive Multimodal Analytics at Scale
papers.metaphor: A vintage television drawn as an exploded engineering diagram — screen, dials, speaker and tubes floating apart in mid-air — with neat streams of tiny faces, waveforms and symbols flowing from each part into a row of glass specimen jars below.

papers.match: Misinformation Mitigation Praxis: Lessons Learned and Future Directions from Co-Insights
papers.metaphor: A lighthouse built from stacked magnifying glasses sweeping its wide beam across a dark sea dotted with paper boats folded from rumor pages; the boats caught in the beam turn back toward the horizon.

papers.match: Online Knowledge Production in Polarized Political Memes: The Case of Critical Race Theory
papers.metaphor: Two facing printing presses stamping the same picture card in two different color schemes; two crowds collect their versions hot off the press and carry them away in opposite directions, stacking them into two separate leaning towers.

papers.match: Minimizing polarization and disagreement using topic-based timeline algorithms
papers.metaphor: A river split into two churning rapids being gently re-braided into one calm stream by a series of small wooden lock gates, operated by a patient lock-keeper pulling simple levers on a bank-side control post.

papers.match: Unraveling the Dynamics of Television Debates and Social Media Engagement: Insights from an Indian News Show
papers.metaphor: A television studio desk where a heated panel debate throws literal sparks; each spark arcs out into the dark audience, lands on a raised phone, and becomes a small glowing bird that flies up out of frame.

papers.match: Social Media Narratives on Conflict from Northern Syria
papers.metaphor: A cracked stone wall bearing three overlapping weathered posters of the same village landscape painted three different ways — one in flames, one peaceful, one abandoned — with small phone screens embedded in the wall like lit windows.

papers.match: Understanding and combatting misinformation across 16 countries on six continents
papers.metaphor: A ring of sixteen hands of many skin tones around a round table, each holding an identical rumor card up toward one shared candle in the center, the candlelight revealing a checkmark watermark in some cards and a hollow void in others.

papers.match: On the Rise of Fear Speech in Online Social Media
papers.metaphor: A quiet row of houses at night where a thin ribbon of dark smoke slips out of one window, thickening as it drifts house to house and wrapping around door handles like a rope; one window stays warmly lit with its shutters open.

papers.match: Displacement and Return in the Internet Era: How Social Media Captures Migration Decisions in Northern Syria
papers.metaphor: A family walking a long winding road carrying suitcases, following stepping stones that are glowing phone screens laid in the dirt; far ahead the road loops back toward a small house on the horizon with one lit window.

papers.metaphor: Three kitchen tables in three homes — one with a tiny espresso cup, one with a steel chai tumbler, one with pan dulce on a plate — connected across rooftops by a single telephone wire strung with paper chat bubbles like laundry.

papers.metaphor: A carnival high-striker game where a whole team of identical small figures swing their hammers in perfect unison, sending the puck up the tower toward a bell shaped like a little bird, while a lone fairgoer watches suspiciously.

papers.match: Decentralized yet Unifying: Digital Media and Solidarity in Hong Kong's Anti-Extradition Movement
papers.metaphor: A dense night skyline where hundreds of separate phone flashlights shine from balconies and windows, their beams converging overhead into the shape of one huge glowing umbrella sheltering the city.

papers.metaphor: A postal sorter at a conveyor belt of sealed padlocked envelopes, weighing each one on a small hand scale without opening it; the heavy oily-dark ones tip the scale and drop through a trapdoor into a bin below.

papers.metaphor: Two water mills on opposite banks of one river, each wheel scooping up chat bubbles from the current and pouring them into its own village's cistern through bamboo channels, the two streams never mixing.

papers.match: Tiplines to Combat Misinformation on Encrypted Platforms: A Case Study of the 2019 Indian Election on WhatsApp
papers.metaphor: An old wooden counter with a brass bell and a tip jar where people queue to drop in folded suspicious notes; behind the counter a clerk with an eyeshade unfolds one under a desk lamp, holding two rubber stamps — one with a check, one with a cross.

papers.metaphor: A globe wearing a paper face mask, orbited like satellites by a ring of clipboards with checkbox forms, each clipboard trailing a tiny pencil on a string; a few checkmarks drift in space between them.

papers.metaphor: Backstage at a theater, a crew of stagehands hoists a giant papier-mache flame up through the stage floor with ropes and pulleys, while the audience out front applauds the flame as though it had risen on its own.

papers.metaphor: A telephone switchboard operator plugging cables between rows of speech bubbles filled with different abstract squiggle scripts; wherever two bubbles say the same thing, the pair lights up in the same color.

papers.metaphor: One tree drawn across four seasons in a single panorama: a few birds on the sapling, a great chattering flock at its summer peak, thinning in autumn, and a handful of loyal birds on the bare winter branch.

papers.metaphor: A string of paper lanterns strung between village houses at night; most lanterns glow warmly, but a dark flame jumps from lantern to lantern along the string, and a child watches it approach from a doorway.

papers.metaphor: A breakfast table split down the middle by a tall newspaper wall; two readers on either side sip from identical coffee cups, each facing a completely different front page printed on their side of the same wall.

papers.metaphor: A dusk skyline of domes and minarets with a single paper kite flying above it, the kite's long tail made of chained chat bubbles carrying tiny masks and pill shapes from rooftop to rooftop.

papers.metaphor: A boutique window display where a glamorous mannequin holds a phone taking a selfie; a tiny price tag hangs discreetly from the phone on a thread, and a detective's magnifying glass in the corner of the window enlarges the tag.

papers.metaphor: Two identical mannequins side by side on twin weighing scales, one dressed in dark solids and one in bold horizontal stripes; the two scale needles point to different numbers while a puzzled observer scratches their head.

papers.metaphor: Two staircases built from computer keyboards climbing toward the same glowing monitor at the summit — one staircase crowded with climbers, the other nearly empty — with a small bridge under construction between them.

papers.metaphor: A bucket brigade of round chat-bubble characters passing water pails toward a bonfire of burning rumor pages — but arriving as the fire has mostly burned down, embers still glowing under the smoke.

papers.metaphor: A wet photograph pinned to a clothesline, dripping ink; each drip lands and blooms into a smaller copy of the same photo, and the copies grow subtly more distorted with every row down.

papers.metaphor: One great banyan tree whose separate branches bloom in different colors of leaves, each branch's leaves patterned with a different abstract script squiggle, all sharing a single root system woven from chat bubbles.

papers.metaphor: A relay race of chat-bubble runners passing a flaming baton down a track; a referee's white-gloved hand places a hurdle in front of every fifth runner, and the flame visibly shrinks at each hurdle it clears.
